mission.title: Install ModelPack Model CSI Driver on Kubernetes
mission.description: ModelPack is an open standard (hosted under LF Projects) for packaging, distributing and running AI/ML models as OCI artifacts. This mission installs the Model CSI Driver — a Kubernetes CSI driver from the ModelPack project that mounts ModelPack-format OCI model artifacts as volumes directly into pods. It is compatible with older Kubernetes versions and natively supports P2P-accelerated distribution. After install, pods can reference models by OCI reference via inline CSI volumes with driver `model.csi.modelpack.org`.

steps.title: Create a values file for registry auth and storage
steps.description:
Create `values-custom.yaml` with the CSI driver's working directory and any private registry credentials it needs to pull model artifacts. `rootDir` must be writable on each node and have enough disk for the models you intend to mount:
```yaml
config:
  rootDir: /var/lib/model-csi
  registryAuths:
    registry.example.com:
      # base64(username:password)
      auth: dXNlcm5hbWU6cGFzc3dvcmQ=
      serverscheme: https
image:
  repository: ghcr.io/modelpack/model-csi-driver
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  tag: v0.1.2
```
If you only pull from public registries, omit the `registryAuths` block.

steps.title: Install the Model CSI Driver via Helm
steps.description:
The chart is published as an OCI artifact at `ghcr.io/modelpack/charts/model-csi-driver`. Install it into the `model-csi` namespace:
```bash
helm upgrade --install model-csi-driver \
    oci://ghcr.io/modelpack/charts/model-csi-driver \
    --version 0.1.2 \
    --namespace model-csi \
    --create-namespace \
    -f values-custom.yaml
```
The driver runs as a DaemonSet, so one pod is scheduled per node that matches the chart's node selector.

steps.title: Verify the DaemonSet pods are running
steps.description:
Confirm a driver pod is Ready on every eligible node:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n model-csi -o wide
kubectl get daemonset -n model-csi
```
Also confirm the CSI driver is registered with the cluster:
```bash
kubectl get csidriver model.csi.modelpack.org
```

steps.title: Mount a model artifact into a test pod
steps.description:
The driver uses inline CSI volumes referenced directly in the pod spec — no PV/PVC needed. Replace the `reference` with an OCI URL to a ModelPack-format artifact you have access to:
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: model-inference-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - name: inference-server
    image: ubuntu:24.04
    command: ["sleep", "infinity"]
    volumeMounts:
    - name: model-volume
      mountPath: /model
      readOnly: true
  volumes:
  - name: model-volume
    csi:
      driver: model.csi.modelpack.org
      volumeAttributes:
        model.csi.modelpack.org/reference: "registry.example.com/models/qwen3-0.6b:latest"
```
Apply it and verify the model files appear under `/model` inside the container:
```bash
kubectl apply -f model-inference-pod.yaml
kubectl exec model-inference-pod -- ls /model
```
Use [modctl](https://github.com/modelpack/modctl) to build and push ModelPack artifacts if you need a test model.

resolution.summary: A successful install shows one model-csi-driver DaemonSet pod per eligible node in the `model-csi` namespace, all reporting Ready. The `model.csi.modelpack.org` CSIDriver object exists, and a test pod can mount an OCI model artifact via an inline CSI volume and read the model files under its mount path.

uninstall.title: Delete pods that reference the CSI volume
uninstall.description:
Inline CSI volumes are tied to pod lifecycle. Delete any pods that still mount `model.csi.modelpack.org` volumes before removing the driver so volume unpublish runs cleanly:
```bash
kubectl delete pod model-inference-pod
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the chart, which removes the DaemonSet and the CSIDriver object it registered:
```bash
helm uninstall model-csi-driver --namespace model-csi
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the dedicated namespace to clean up remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace model-csi
```

upgrade.title: Update the chart reference and image tag
upgrade.description: Bump the version in your upgrade command and, if pinning the image, update the `image.tag` in `values-custom.yaml` to match. Check the releases page at https://github.com/modelpack/model-csi-driver/releases for the latest tag.

upgrade.title: Run the Helm upgrade
upgrade.description:
Re-run the same install command with the new version:
```bash
helm upgrade --install model-csi-driver \
    oci://ghcr.io/modelpack/charts/model-csi-driver \
    --version <new-version> \
    --namespace model-csi \
    -f values-custom.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Verify the rollout
upgrade.description:
Confirm the DaemonSet rolls out cleanly to all nodes:
```bash
kubectl rollout status daemonset/model-csi-driver -n model-csi
kubectl get pods -n model-csi
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod stuck in Pending or ContainerCreating
troubleshooting.description:
Describe the workload pod to see why CSI volume attachment is failing, then check the driver logs on the same node:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name>
kubectl logs -n model-csi -l app.kubernetes.io/name=model-csi-driver -c model-csi-driver --tail=200
```
Common causes: registry auth missing from `values-custom.yaml`, model reference typo, or `rootDir` not writable on the node.

troubleshooting.title: CSIDriver object not found
troubleshooting.description:
If `kubectl get csidriver model.csi.modelpack.org` returns NotFound, the chart install did not complete. Check the Helm release status and the DaemonSet events:
```bash
helm status model-csi-driver -n model-csi
kubectl get events -n model-csi --sort-by=.lastTimestamp
```

troubleshooting.title: Pull failures from private registries
troubleshooting.description:
Auth errors when mounting a model volume usually mean the registry host in `values-custom.yaml` doesn't exactly match the host in the OCI reference, or the base64 auth string is malformed. Regenerate it with:
```bash
printf '%s' 'username:password' | base64
```
and re-run `helm upgrade` with the updated values file.

troubleshooting.title: Disk pressure on nodes
troubleshooting.description: The driver caches pulled model layers under `config.rootDir` (default `/var/lib/model-csi`). Large models can fill the node disk. Point `rootDir` at a dedicated volume with sufficient capacity, or clean unused artifacts from that directory on affected nodes.

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster (ModelPack's Model CSI Driver supports older versions; >=1.20 is a safe baseline), Helm 3.8+ (required for OCI chart pulls), and kubectl configured against the target cluster. Nodes must have writable storage at `config.rootDir` (default `/var/lib/model-csi`) with enough disk space to cache pulled model artifacts. Private model registries require credentials to be supplied via the values file.
